word_list.pronunciation: dɹˈaɪv ˌɛsbˈiː kɹˈeɪzi ɔːɹ mˈæd ɔːɹ ɪnsˈeɪn ɔːɹ nˈʌts

senses.definition: to do something that makes someone extremely upset, annoyed, or angry

examples.x: The constant noise from the construction work next door is driving me crazy.
The repetitive, loud music from the neighbor's party is driving me nuts.
Dealing with the never-ending traffic in this city can drive anyone crazy.

senses.definition: to enter a state of deep unconsciousness from which a person cannot be easily awakened

examples.x: After the accident, the injured motorcyclist fell into a coma.
The patient's condition deteriorated, and they fell into a coma.
The doctor explained the risks of surgery, including the possibility of falling into a coma.
The medical team worked tirelessly to revive the patient who had fallen into a coma.
He suffered a massive stroke and subsequently fell into a coma for several weeks.

senses.definition: to reveal something surprising or unexpected to someone

examples.x: The children sprung a surprise on their parents by cleaning the house while they were away.
The team sprung a surprise by unveiling a new product at the conference.

senses.definition: to press one's teeth against the lip as a reaction to emotion, pain, or to prevent oneself from saying something

examples.x: She had to bite her lip to prevent herself from saying something hurtful during the argument.
The child bit his lip to keep from crying after falling down.
Even though she was nervous, she bit her lip and gave a confident speech.

senses.definition: to have good intentions, with the desire to do something positive or helpful, even if the results may not align with those intentions

examples.x: She may have made mistakes, but she genuinely meant well in her efforts to support the community.
They meant well when they tried to surprise you, even if it didn't turn out as planned.
The parents meant well when they gave their child advice, even if it wasn't what the child wanted to hear.
Sometimes people's good intentions lead them to make decisions that don't work out, but they still mean well.
It's important to remember that he means well and is trying to make amends for his actions.

senses.definition: to quickly come to the aid or support of another person when they are under attack, criticism, or facing a difficult situation

examples.x: When her friend was falsely accused, she sprang to her defense with evidence to prove her innocence.

senses.definition: to earn the confidence, belief, or reliance of another person

examples.x: It took time and honesty to win her trust after a period of misunderstandings.

word_list.pronunciation: stˈænd ðə sˈaɪt ʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to tolerate the presence of someone or something one dislikes very much

examples.x: Every time I see that messy room, I can't stand the sight of it.
The awful smell from the garbage can made him unable to stand the sight of it.
John couldn't stand the sight of his rude coworker any longer.
His unwavering dedication to his craft allows him to stand the sight of countless hours of rehearsal and still maintain his passion for dance.

senses.definition: to share similarities in appearance, characteristics, or qualities

examples.x: The two paintings bear a resemblance in their use of color and composition.
The newly discovered species bears a resemblance to a previously known but extinct animal.

senses.definition: to earn confidence and belief from another person in one's honesty, reliability, and intentions

examples.x: He gained my trust when he consistently kept his promises.
They met at a coffee shop and quickly fell in love.
After many years of friendship, they finally fell in love with each other.
The couple fell in love during a summer vacation in Italy.
She didn't expect to fall in love while studying abroad, but it happened.
Falling in love can be a beautiful and life-changing experience.

senses.definition: to treat someone to meals and drinks, typically in a luxurious or extravagant manner

examples.x: The couple enjoyed a romantic evening when he decided to wine and dine her at an upscale restaurant.
Business partners often wine and dine prospective clients to build stronger relationships.
To celebrate their anniversary, they decided to wine and dine at a five-star hotel.
He loves to wine and dine his guests, showcasing his culinary skills.
While negotiating the deal, they were wining and dining the international partners.

senses.definition: to perform exceptionally well or brilliantly in a specific situation or task

examples.x: The team captain played a blinder in the final match, leading the team to victory.
She played a blinder during the important presentation and impressed the clients.
Despite the challenging conditions, the golfer played a blinder and won the tournament.
In the game's critical moments, the goalkeeper played a blinder by making several crucial saves.
Her performance in the job interview was exceptional; she truly played a blinder and got the position.

senses.definition: to make a formal vow to get married to someone

examples.x: In a traditional ceremony, they plighted their troth before family and friends, pledging their lifelong commitment.
As they exchanged rings, they symbolically plighted their troth, sealing their commitment to each other.
The couple decided to plight their troth privately, exchanging vows in a quiet ceremony by the seaside.

senses.definition: to feel one has enough energy and mental capacity to be able to do something
used to express intense emotional states like amusement, embarrassment, shame, etc. that one is going through

examples.x: I almost died of embarrassment when I tripped in front of everyone.
She died of laughter at the comedian's jokes.
He'll die of shock when he hears the news.
I could have died of frustration dealing with that computer issue.
I thought I would die of boredom during that long lecture.

senses.definition: to direct one's focus or concentration toward something specific

examples.x: The loud noise in the distance caused everyone to turn their attention toward it.
The unexpected announcement caused the media to turn their attention to the small town.
The teacher used a bell to signal when it was time for the students to turn their attention back to their desks.
Her sudden outburst made everyone in the room turn their attention toward her.

senses.definition: to suddenly and uncontrollably express strong emotions, often in a negative way

examples.x: Having a fit at work is unprofessional and can harm relationships.
She had a fit of rage when she discovered her phone was missing.
She had a fit when her car wouldn't start.
He threw a fit when he learned the event was canceled.
She threw a fit when she couldn't find her phone.

senses.definition: to attract or receive attention, often by means of effort, achievement, or significance

examples.x: The new movie garnered attention for its innovative storytelling and stunning visuals.
The artist's latest exhibition has garnered attention from critics and art enthusiasts alike.
The controversial remarks made by the politician quickly garnered attention from the media.
The fashion designer's bold designs have garnered attention on the international runway.
The athlete's remarkable performance at the Olympics garnered attention from sports fans worldwide.
